message:
perf(storage): batch ensured-daily-stats key check under one lock acquisition

The hot path that decides whether the daily-stats UPDATE can short-circuit
was acquiring \`self.ensured_daily_stats_keys.lock()\` once per key inside
\`cache_keys.iter().all(...)\`. With the typical 3-target write fan-out
that's 3 mutex acquisitions per row update — a tight, easy win.

- New \`FrankenStorage::daily_stats_keys_already_ensured<const N: usize>\`
  takes the slice of N keys, locks the cache once, and runs the
  \`iter().all(contains)\` check while holding that single guard.
- \`franken_update_ensured_daily_stats_targets_in_tx\` switches to the
  batched call. The single per-key helper
  \`daily_stats_key_already_ensured\` is preserved for callers that still
  check one key at a time.

Behavior is unchanged: same boolean answer, same short-circuit path, just
with O(1) lock acquisitions per call instead of O(N).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
